World Cup 2023

RESULT
4th Match (D/N), Delhi, October 07, 2023, ICC Cricket World Cup
PrevNext
South Africa
428/5
Sri Lanka
(44.5/50 ov, T:429) 326

South Africa won by 102 runs

Player Of The Match
Aiden Markram
, SA
106 (54)
Summary
Scorecard
Report
Commentary
Stats
Overs
Table
Blog
News
Videos
Photos
Showing Newest
SL
Full commentary

Not the perfect game but a hugely entertaining one. South Africa have stepped up big time in their opener. Sri Lanka had their moments but will need bigger pushes if they are to go big without a few key players. Anyway, all teams apart from India and Australia have played a game now. Big one, as Sambit Bal writes. Hope to see you for it. For now, goodbye from me, Ekanth, my report-writing co-commentator, Madushka Balasuriya, and our scorers, Chandan Duorah and Thilak Ram.

Temba Bavuma, South Africa captain: Happy. We wanted to win and did that. As a batter, nothing to find fault in. Asked for a clinical performance with ball. Didn't get that but individual performances were good. Probably a blessing in disguise that we lost the toss. Ball came on to the bat better when the lights came on. The point was to adjust with the conditions today. We had a certain perception, thought it'd be a bit more variable but it didn't. Probably didn't adjust early enough, to their main batter being Mendis. Kesh took the pace off and was very good. Maybe need to add spin options, these are the learnings we have to take. Challenge would be adapting there again. Quinton will be fine, didn't field but will be fine.

Dasun Shanaka, Sri Lanka captain: I expected a high-scoring game. Rassie, Quinny, Markram got great hundreds. We were missing lengths regularly, execution wasn't good. When we play again, we need to change that. We expected to keep them to 350-370, we thought it'd be manageable given Asalanka and Mendis' form but we couldn't deal with the extra runs. Tough one to manage things while missing three key bowlers. But you know that's part of the game. I'm happy with the positive intent with bat; first ten overs with ball was good too. Need to sharpen up for the next game.

Aiden Markram, POTM: Says his fastest WC ODI ton makes him feels fantastic. That it comes off some days, doesn't on others. That it's fantastic today. I know what's expected of me as a batter. We're marrying being positive with being smart. Great how it's coming together in the last 12 months. Initially, you want to get a feel for the wicket and find out the options you can take and then back it, regardless of the result, and trying things out that may not be in your repertoire. Wicket played well, ball travelled, was an entertaining game. Nice to be on the right side of the result. Really happy with how it went tonight.

Mustafa Moudi : "So far in the first 4 matches of the edition have alternative results - Chasing sides won the 1st and 3rd ODI while Defending sides won the 2nd and the 4th Match !!"

M.: "Great fight by SL. Only difference was the 3 batters who got 50's for SA converted to 100's whereas SL's 3 batters with 50's couldn't convert here."

10:17pm A 102-run win. Margin might be smaller than what South Africa would've expected a while ago. But Shanaka's 68 dragged the innings further, so did Rajitha's cameo. Some mitigation comes from the fact that 754 runs were scored in the 94.5 overs today and that all of South Africa's bowlers struck. Mendis was exceptional in the powerplay, he kept going despite Jansen getting both openers in the phase. The pick-up shots were a treat. But he fell, tickling a cut off Rabada to the keeper. Coetzee and Maharaj then chipped in to put Sri Lanka under deeper pressure. But the last five wickets cost South Africa 176 runs. Shanaka found some form as well. South Africa, hence, didn't get as big a net-run-rate spike but still managed a win over a hundred runs.

44.5
W
Rabada to Pathirana, OUT

Bails fly. Done and dusted. Pathirana made room and tried to whack it over wide long-on. Cross seamer landed around fourth and hit the outer-half of off stump

Matheesha Pathirana b Rabada 5 (16b 1x4 0x6 25m) SR: 31.25

Coetzee to Madushanka, FOUR runs

•
Coetzee to Madushanka, no run

Full on the pads. Pace-on. Beats the flick and deflects off the front pad

Back over the wicket. Slip in

43.4
W
Coetzee to Rajitha, OUT

Pulled in the air... finds Markram at deep-mid! Slower one, 119ks, following the batter as he made room. He hit it hard but the follow-through ended around just over head and didn't go close to the shoulder. Good cameo from Rajitha

Jansen to Rajitha, SIX runs

Rajitha with a 180-degree pull. He swivelled as he no-look pulled the shortish ball flat over, wow, mid-on! Jansen turned right after it made contact

4
Jansen to Rajitha, FOUR runs

What a shot! Clears the front leg and drives the fullish ball outside off over straight mid-off. Controlled follow-through without sacrificing power. Great hit through the line

42.1
1w
Jansen to Rajitha, 1 wide

Makes room and swings across. Slower one outside off beats the swing. Called wide to Rajitha's surprise

Advice: "I'm saying this for the third time. SA's bowling isn't what it used to be. They always bowl well in parts, but the overall performance is not that impressive." Think it was okay. The use of slower ones as the innings progressed shows good adaptability too

Jansen to Rajitha, FOUR runs

Makes room and gets followed. Goes through with the pull over midwicket to bring up Sri Lanka's 300. Was on a back of length, scrambled seam. Came at a good pace but Rajitha's reactions are great

40.5
•
Jansen to Rajitha, no run

Yorker on middle. Makes room and jams it to the left of the bowler, who slides out his left foot to stop it.

John: "This game is deceptive in that it hides the fact that the South African bowling attack isn't playing that well. There have been many wayward balls, many sixes and a better side will take them apart." Yeah, that and the drop catches.

40.4
4
Jansen to Rajitha, FOUR runs

Flat bats himself out of shape but clears mid-off. Not that short a ball, Rajitha backed away and swung. Got enough. Looks like there's an issue with his left shoulder. Physio comes in for a check. All good

Maharaj to Rajitha, no run

Lbw appeal, turned down. Full ball on leg stump from around the wicket, beat the flick and hit pad. 85kph. SA chat and go for the review. Might as well go for it with two in the bank. Off stump fully visible as the ball beats inside edge and hits just under the knee-roll. Impact is umpire's call, so it's going down

4

Dilan: "Shanaka should now just treat this innings as a net session. If he plays himself into form, that could bolster Sri Lanka's campaign more than actually winning the match." Yes, he has been trying but he's not getting any confident shots, off the middle. Worth trying to change gears now. He gets a new bat

With that I need to get prepping for my report. Here's Ekanth to take you through the rest of this game.

32.2
W
Coetzee to Wellalage, OUT

And is this the game?! Wellalage nicks through to the keeper off this length ball, and Sri Lanka's slim chances are now surely all but extinguished

Dunith Wellalage c †Klaasen b Coetzee 0 (1b 0x4 0x6 2m) SR: 0

Tinura: "Shanaka may have stayed for 35 balls now - but le me say this - I have never seen any batter who looked as out of form as him. His shots, timing, bat swing, body language - basically everything about him - seems really disoriented..."

32.1
1
Coetzee to Shanaka, 1 run

back of a length on off, opens the face and runs it down to third man

Wellalage enters the fray. This is now the last recognised batting pair

Anupam: "Every cricket fan would love Shanaka to do well tonight. The way he marshalled his troops during troubled time in his country and brought happiness to his people is absolutely amazing. Nothing unites a country more than a sport. "

length on the pads, worked around the corner for one

Rabada to Asalanka, FOUR runs

whipped behind square! Nice use of the wrists as he takes this short ball and clips it to backward square leg

Yasir Awan: "Just do it SL, keep playing like a T20 game now...."

Vipin Chauhan : "Double the score which was at 30 over mark , and Sri Lanka at home. Very simple "

Thanks Ekanth. So lots of comments coming through on SL doubling after 30, but the tongue in cheek nature of those comments shows just how incredible South Africa's hitting was

end of over 3013 runs

Target still looks miles far but at least Sri Lanka's wickets aren't tumbling. South Africa will need to improve their fielding after this drinks break, which means it's time for Madushka to return

29.5
6
Jansen to Shanaka, SIX runs

Shortish ball is pulled. Top edge goes wide to the left of deep fine leg. Lucky six.

29.4
1
Jansen to Asalanka, 1 run

Risky run to Maharaj at cover, no direct hit at the non-striker's end though. Would've been close had it hit. Fullish ball outside off was defended to the right of the fielder, who did well to run past the ball before using the left hand to try and hit the bowler's end and catch Asalanka by surprise

29.3
•
Jansen to Asalanka, no run

Pulled to the left of midwicket, in the ring. Markram scoots along and stops it on the second bounce. Ball was short just outside off. timing of the shot was good

29.2
•
Jansen to Asalanka, no run

Low full toss on middle and off, pushed to mid-off

29.1
6
Jansen to Asalanka, SIX runs

Launched over mid-on, just about clears the ropes. Mid-on was in and Jansen went on the fuller side. Not particularly a driving length but Asalanka drove it from just outside off with decent enough timing

end of over 295 runs
SL: 201/5CRR: 6.93 • RRR: 10.85 • Need 228 runs from 21 overs
Dasun Shanaka12 (28b)
Charith Asalanka62 (54b 6x4 3x6)
Kagiso Rabada 6-0-38-1
Marco Jansen 6-0-51-2
28.6
•
Rabada to Shanaka, no run

Tries to dab it fine, misses. Length ball on fifth at 136ks. Shanaka's feet froze as he tried to guide it. Like someone getting back in form, he looks tentative often

28.5
1
Rabada to Asalanka, 1 run

Slower bouncer, around chest-height, pulled to deep square leg

28.4
1
Rabada to Shanaka, 1 run

Pulls the shortish ball outside off at 136ks, to deep square leg. Fifty stand and Sri Lanka's 200 up

28.3
•
Rabada to Shanaka, no run

Back of length on off. Clips it while going square-on, to midwicket

28.2
•
Rabada to Shanaka, no run

Beat the outside edge. Test length, just outside off. Shanaka had a poke and missed it

28.1
1
Rabada to Asalanka, 1 run

Makes room by taking the front foot well out the way, and pulls the free hit straight to Hendricks at deep square leg

28.1
2nb
Rabada to Shanaka, (no ball) 1 run

Length on the thigh pad, clipped to deep fine leg. No-ball siren rings. Replay shows Rabada was well over the line

end of over 2810 runs
SL: 196/5CRR: 7.00 • RRR: 10.59 • Need 233 runs from 22 overs
Charith Asalanka60 (52b 6x4 3x6)
Dasun Shanaka10 (23b)
Marco Jansen 6-0-51-2
Keshav Maharaj 8-0-43-1

Mo: "Jansen should be on 5 now" This came in a few minutes back and I hadn't posted it because there weren't chances off him. But this over brought about two

27.6
4
Jansen to Asalanka, FOUR runs

Behind the keeper, twenty-odd metres further too... how'd it go there? Ball was shortish outside off, Asalanka shaped for the pull before looking to withdraw the bat. Ball hit bat, top edge, and flew up and over

27.5
•
Jansen to Asalanka, no run

Blocks the length ball in front

27.4
4
Jansen to Asalanka, FOUR runs

Top edge off the pull, and Andile drops one now. He was close to the ropes, was worried he'd step over, was leaning to the right as he tried to take the catch. Got both hands on it but the lack of balance popped the ball out the hand. One-bounce four, although he signalled six. Shortish ball just outside off, Asalanka tried to pull it across, got the top edge

27.3
1
Jansen to Shanaka, 1 run

Wide on a length, slapped to deep point. 125ks

27.2
1
Jansen to Asalanka, 1 run

Tries to go big but inside edges it onto pad. Full around off, 140ks, tried to whip it across. Got it towards square leg

27.1
•
Jansen to Asalanka, no run

Fullish ball on middle, 137ks, blocked towards mid-on

end of over 277 runs

•
Maharaj to Shanaka, no run

Full on leg, blocked in front. 87ks

26.5
1
Maharaj to Asalanka, 1 run

Length on leg, he went across and pulled it to deep-backward square

26.4
1
Maharaj to Shanaka, 1 run

Down the pitch, gets close to the pitch and pumps it, along the ground, to long-on

26.3
1
Maharaj to Asalanka, 1 run

Length outside off, goes close to the ball, then jerks back as he steers it to short third. Asalanka brings up fifty. Raises his bat but subdued celebrations overall

26.2
4
Maharaj to Asalanka, FOUR runs

Length ball lands on leg and spins in further, easy leg glance for Asalanka. Short fine dives to the diagonal left but ball beats him

26.1
•
Maharaj to Asalanka, no run

Dances down, gets it on the low full toss. Drives to the right of cover. Markram there, stops it with a dive

end of over 263 runs

•
Jansen to Shanaka, no run

Yorker is pushed to cover

25.5
•
Jansen to Shanaka, no run

Back of length just outside off, tries to guide it but gets beaten

25.4
1
Jansen to Asalanka, 1 run

Pulled in the air... van der Dussen drops it at deep square leg! Asalanka had given up and stopped running, the batters had to scamper. Replay shows the ball went through VDD's fingers. Ball was on a back of length, Asalanka couldn't cope with the bounce, getting big on him. One hand came off as the ball went off the top edge

25.3
1
Jansen to Shanaka, 1 run

Slap-like pull forces the bouncer, around off, to deep square leg

25.2
•
Jansen to Shanaka, no run

Length on middle and off, 133ks, defended to cover point

25.1
1
Jansen to Asalanka, 1 run

Length on fourth, clipped to square leg

Amin: "Can we see Markram? He can do no wrong today." Even better. Jansen's back

Misfield at cover, think the batters thought of a run but they're not taking on Miller. Ball sat up off a length and was cut after making room

•

Full on middle and off, stays leg side and punches away from the body to cover

•

Tries to nudge the full ball square but leading edges it. Rolls to the bowler it does

1

23.2
1w
Ngidi to Asalanka, 1 wide

Bouncer is high, over the batter and is let go

23.1

W
Maharaj to Dhananjaya, OUT

Slices a cut to Phehlukhwayo at backward point. The fielder moved to the right, slid his knee across and took it low. Ball was on a length outside off, bounced more than DDS expected, spun a bit and the around-the-wicket angle came in play too

19.5
6
Coetzee to Asalanka, SIX runs

19.4
4
Coetzee to Asalanka, FOUR runs

19.3
•
Coetzee to Asalanka, no run

1

18.3
1
Maharaj to Dhananjaya, 1 run

The timing on the sweeps is gradually increasing, still not 100%. Full ball was dragged across to the outfield, deep square leg ran to the left and cut it off

18.2
4
Maharaj to Dhananjaya, FOUR runs

This time the comes in straight, DDS goes for the hard sweep again, Underedges it, ball goes fine, to the left of the keeper

18.1
•
Maharaj to Dhananjaya, no run

Stumping appeal, sent upstairs. The slow, 80kph, stock ball was fullish. Landed around off and ragged away sharply. Beat the sweep shot. DDS was on one knee, back-knee grounded, as he went for the shot. Missed it and pushed the shoe back in the crease as Klaasen whipped out the bails.

Slip in. Still around is Maharaj

end of over 1811 runs

1

•

samraat: "I enjoyed Kusal Mendis' knock until it lasted. Always knew 428 was out of reach. But what happened to Kotla's pitch? I think it would be low and spin." --- Yup, much more grass than anyone expected.

13.2
W
Coetzee to Samarawickrama, OUT

Coetzee strikes with just his second ball! Sadeera was looking to work this onside but it's the cross seamer on a back of a length. It sits up a bit in the surface and Sadeera is early on the stroke, and gets a leading edge. It pops up barely past the edge of the pitch towards mid-on, but Jansen makes up the ground in a flash to grab this

Sadeera Samarawickrama c Jansen b Coetzee 23 (19b 3x4 1x6 22m) SR: 121.05
See all photos
13.1
•
Coetzee to Samarawickrama, no run

back of a length, 140kph, on the stumps. Defended to mid-on

Janith : "Mendis did his job. Now the middle over has to follow him "

1
Rabada to Samarawickrama, 1 run

good length on off, driven on the up into the covers for one

1

full on the pads, clipped down to fine leg

Lasan Rashmika: "Meanwhile, Shanaka is forcing Danajaya and Asalanka to change their names to Charith de Asalanka and Danajaya ver der Silva"

12.4
W
Rabada to Mendis, OUT

And Rabada gets the big one! Rabada is ecstatic and so he should be. This was back of a length just outside off, 140kph, and it bounced a touch more than Mendis expected. He looks for the cut but edges it near the shoulder of the bat for Klaasen to gleefully accept

Kusal Mendis c †Klaasen b Rabada 76 (42b 4x4 8x6 55m) SR: 180.95

Rabada to Samarawickrama, 1 leg bye

back of a length, nips in towards the midriff. They run the leg bye as it deflects towards point

MAB: "Mendis needs to slow it down after his 100. He needs to be there at least till 40th over. He needs to gather more energy before his final onslaught." --- Bascially he needs to do it all, yeah?

end of over 107 runs
SL: 94/2CRR: 9.40 • RRR: 8.37 • Need 335 runs from 40 overs
Kusal Mendis72 (35b 4x4 8x6)
Sadeera Samarawickrama12 (7b 1x4 1x6)
Keshav Maharaj 1-0-7-0
Kagiso Rabada 2-0-20-0
9.6
•
Maharaj to Mendis, no run

full back of a length on off gloves this towards short third as he looks to leave it

9.5
1
Maharaj to Samarawickrama, 1 run

full on off, driven through extra cover

9.4
6
Maharaj to Samarawickrama, SIX runs

Samarawickrama joins the party! Uses his feet, doesn't quite get to the pitch of it but he gets enough to just about clear the midwicket ropes

9.3
•
Maharaj to Samarawickrama, no run

looks to go straighter this time, drifts down leg

9.2
•
Maharaj to Samarawickrama, no run

short and wide, but cut straight to cover. Again some significant turn

9.1
•
Maharaj to Samarawickrama, no run

And some turn immediately. Good length on off, 82kph, turns past the outside edge

Maharaj with spin now

Khalid: "If Mendish brings the RRR down to 6 before losing his wicket, SL will have a great chance to chase this down" --- Still quite some way for that, but liking the optimism Khalid.

end of over 916 runs
SL: 87/2CRR: 9.66 • RRR: 8.34 • Need 342 runs from 41 overs
Kusal Mendis72 (34b 4x4 8x6)
Sadeera Samarawickrama5 (2b 1x4)
Kagiso Rabada 2-0-20-0
Marco Jansen 4-0-38-2
8.6
6
Rabada to Mendis, SIX runs

It's raining sixes! This was short and pulled in front of square this time. Mendis is on a mission!

8.5
2
Rabada to Mendis, 2 runs

back of a length on the stumps, tucked square. Should just be one but a misfield allows for the second

8.4
6
Rabada to Mendis, SIX runs

Six more to the counter! This was short and Mendis just helps it on its way, pulled cleanly over fine leg

8.3
•
Rabada to Mendis, no run

pace-off on the stumps, defended

Sonal : "Mendis needs support. Very badly "

8.2
1
Rabada to Samarawickrama, 1 run

back of a length on middle and leg, worked behind square leg for one

8.1
1
Rabada to Mendis, 1 run

back of a length, angled in to the body. Lowers his hands and guides this to short third for one

end of over 813 runs • 1 wicket
SL: 71/2CRR: 8.87 • RRR: 8.52 • Need 358 runs from 42 overs
Sadeera Samarawickrama4 (1b 1x4)
Kusal Mendis57 (29b 4x4 6x6)
Marco Jansen 4-0-38-2
Kagiso Rabada 1-0-4-0
7.6
4
Jansen to Samarawickrama, FOUR runs

Clipped to fine leg! Strayed on the pads and this is flicked away with ease

7.5
W
Jansen to Perera, OUT

Jansen sends the bails on a flight. Length ball came back in and was too fast for Perera, who tried to block it straight. His upper body fell over, the ball snuck through and Jansen has two. Sid Monga, at the ground, says the bail reached the third man boundary

Kusal Perera b Jansen 7 (15b 1x4 0x6 42m) SR: 46.66
7.4
4
Jansen to Perera, FOUR runs

Length at the body. Jansen's height allows easy back-foot play for Perera. Pulled it over forward square leg

7.3
•
Jansen to Perera, no run

Tries to pick it up and deposit over midwicket but the fullish ball zipped outside off

7.2
1
Jansen to Mendis, 1 run

Not too short but rises around waist-height to Mendis, who pulls to deep square leg

7.1
4
Jansen to Mendis, FOUR runs

Overpitched and it's clipped through midwicket. It was on middle, Mendis' front leg made way for the bat to come through

end of over 74 runs
SL: 58/1CRR: 8.28 • RRR: 8.62 • Need 371 runs from 43 overs
Kusal Perera3 (12b)
Kusal Mendis52 (27b 3x4 6x6)
Kagiso Rabada 1-0-4-0
Marco Jansen 3-0-25-1
6.6
•
Rabada to Perera, no run
6.5
2
Rabada to Perera, 2 runs
6.4
•
Rabada to Perera, no run
6.3
1
Rabada to Mendis, 1 run

good length on the stumps, opens the face and runs it through point for one

6.2
1
Rabada to Perera, 1 run

Misjudged completely! A massive let off for Sri Lanka! This was struck straight up in the air, but Bavuma running in from mid-off doesn't judge the flight of it and it falls between him and Miller at extra cover

6.1
•
Rabada to Perera, no run

back of a length on the stumps, defended towards short extra cover

Mohinish Vigg: "Sl score is 54. Mendis scoring 51 and 3 extras... wow what a dominance "

Tariq: "Mendis might be the first player to score a triple century."

end of over 617 runs
SL: 54/1CRR: 9.00 • RRR: 8.52 • Need 375 runs from 44 overs
Kusal Mendis51 (26b 3x4 6x6)
Kusal Perera0 (7b)
Marco Jansen 3-0-25-1
Lungi Ngidi 3-1-27-0
5.6
•
Jansen to Mendis, no run

squares him up! But Mendis plays with soft hands as the edge bounces towards the slips

5.5
6
Jansen to Mendis, SIX runs

50 up for Mendis in just 25 balls! Again this is full and straight and Mendis flicks this highhhh over deep square leg

See all photos
5.4
4
Jansen to Mendis, FOUR runs

plonked over mid-on! This was a good length on off, angled in from outside. Picks up this drives on the up

5.3
6
Jansen to Mendis, SIX runs

Mendis is dealing in sixes! And this might be the best of the lot! Goes across and just ramp/flicks this over deep backward square

5.2
1lb
Jansen to Perera, 1 leg bye

full on the pads, looks to work it onside but it deflects off the front pad. Perera still not off the mark

5.1
•
Jansen to Perera, no run

good length on off, pushed straight to extra cover

Manish Acharya: "5 overs and only one batsman scoring runs for SriLanka"

end of over 518 runs
SL: 37/1CRR: 7.40 • RRR: 8.71 • Need 392 runs from 45 overs
Kusal Mendis35 (22b 2x4 4x6)
Kusal Perera0 (5b)
Lungi Ngidi 3-1-27-0
Marco Jansen 2-0-9-1
4.6
6
Ngidi to Mendis, SIX runs

Another absolutely magnificent pick-up! Just too full and straight, Mendis steps across and whips this high over deep backward square

4.5
6
Ngidi to Mendis, SIX runs

What a pull shot! This was a length ball, nowhere near short, but Mendis picks up the length so quick. Pulls it high and handsome over deep square leg

4.4
•
Ngidi to Mendis, no run

good length on off, steps across and drives this back straight. Mid-on gets in the way

GM: "This WC is a delight. All teams are heavyweights here. Its just the form of their players that will be the deciding factor." --- Probably the kindest thing said about this Sri Lankan team in about a decade.

4.3
•
Ngidi to Mendis, no run

good length on off, pushed towards point

4.2
6
Ngidi to Mendis, SIX runs

Picked up gloriously! Ngidi strays on the pads and Mendis just flicks his wrists and sends this sailing over deep backward square

4.1
•
Ngidi to Mendis, no run

Angled in towards off from wide of the crease, back of a length. Defended on the back foot

Asrhdeep Mohand: "Natty, dew will be there but not be as high as down south of India where things are more.. humid "

end of over 47 runs
SL: 19/1CRR: 4.75 • RRR: 8.91 • Need 410 runs from 46 overs
Kusal Perera0 (5b)
Kusal Mendis17 (16b 2x4 1x6)
Marco Jansen 2-0-9-1
Lungi Ngidi 2-1-9-0
3.6
•
Jansen to Perera, no run

This one nips in off a good length and nearly catches Perera sitting in his crease. Just about gets the bat down in time

3.5
1
Jansen to Mendis, 1 run

Brings this one in now. Shaped in off a good length. Mendis negotiates it well, drops and runs

3.4
•
Jansen to Mendis, no run

Even better from Jansen! This one pitches on a back of a length on off and seams away. Squares Mendis up as it beats the outside edge

3.3
•
Jansen to Mendis, no run

back of a length, 140kph, angled away from off as Mendis pulls his bat back in late

3.2
6
Jansen to Mendis, SIX runs

Scythed over point! Full and wide and Mendis slashes at this. Gets a good connection and it goes all the way

3.1
•
Jansen to Mendis, no run

Bouncer, and again taken by Klaasen behind the stumps well over his head

Natasha : "Would dew factor play a role tonight??" --- There's a chance, it was one of the reason's SL wanted to bowl first

end of over 39 runs
SL: 12/1CRR: 4.00 • RRR: 8.87 • Need 417 runs from 47 overs
Kusal Mendis10 (11b 2x4)
Kusal Perera0 (4b)
Lungi Ngidi 2-1-9-0
Marco Jansen 1-0-2-1
2.6
1
Ngidi to Mendis, 1 run

full and wide, once more looks to pound a straight drive. Bit more of a horizontal bat on this, but mid-off rolls to his right and gets something on this to keep it to one

Brijesh: "The sublime hitting masterclass from SA is going to terribly affect the SL run rate. Which can become crucial at the later stages, especially with the another heavyweight like ENG going down with a huge runrate deficit"

2.5
•
Ngidi to Mendis, no run

Corker! This one was back of a length in the channel outside off, shaping away late. Has Mendis fishing and missing

2.4
4
Ngidi to Mendis, FOUR runs

Cracking on-drive! This one was too full and straight, Mendis shows the full face of the bat and beats the outstretched hands of mid-on

2.3
•
Ngidi to Mendis, no run

full and straight, looks to push and run towards mid-off but is turned back

2.2
•
Ngidi to Mendis, no run

back of a length, angled in towards off. Mendis looks to dab this behind square but backward point collects

2.1
4
Ngidi to Mendis, FOUR runs

Spanked down the ground! This was in the slot and Mendis obliges, quality straight drive

end of over 22 runs • 1 wicket
SL: 3/1CRR: 1.50 • RRR: 8.87 • Need 426 runs from 48 overs
Kusal Mendis1 (5b)
Kusal Perera0 (4b)
Marco Jansen 1-0-2-1
Lungi Ngidi 1-1-0-0
1.6
1
Jansen to Mendis, 1 run

length ball on the pads, tucked square on the onside for one

1.5
•
Jansen to Mendis, no run

Pacey bouncer, and one for the over signalled. This one passed Mendis just above shoulder height but the keeper took it above his head

1.4
•
Jansen to Mendis, no run

fuller outside off, pushed tentatively towards cover

Matthew: "WOW! That could not have got any closer to the bails without being umpire's call!"

1.3
•
Jansen to Mendis, no run

good length, shaping in once more. Defended

1.3
1w
Jansen to Mendis, 1 wide

More inswing, but this time it's down leg

1.2
•
Jansen to Mendis, no run

Two in two for Jansen... or is it? Another corker from Jansen, swinging in late and this looks plumb. Mendis goes upstairs in hope I expect...but I stand corrected! This is going over the stumps! Jansen's height there getting some extra bounce, as this hit above the knee roll.

1.1
W
Jansen to Nissanka, OUT

Through him first up! What a way to start your spell! This pitched on a good length on off, and then just tailed back in as it cut Nissanka in half before clattering into the top of middle

Pathum Nissanka b Jansen 0 (3b 0x4 0x6 6m) SR: 0
See all photos

Snethemba : "I really don't think SL bowled that badly... SA getting 428 from striking under 5 in the power play shows Aus/Eng gung ho approach to the PP is overrated. Keeping wickets in hand is still the way to go in ODIs."

end of over 1Maiden
SL: 1/0CRR: 1.00 • RRR: 8.73 • Need 428 runs from 49 overs
Kusal Perera0 (4b)
Pathum Nissanka0 (2b)
Lungi Ngidi 1-1-0-0
0.6
•
Ngidi to Perera, no run

Another inducker, strikes the pads as Perera looks to work it onside. It looks to be sliding down leg. Some early movement for Ngidi

0.5
•
Ngidi to Perera, no run

One comes in and the next angles away. Beats KJP on the forwards defence

0.4
•
Ngidi to Perera, no run

Ngidi thinks he has his man as this shapes back and raps KJP on the front pad. That's probably sliding down leg though. Umpire certainly thinks so

0.3
•
Ngidi to Perera, no run

fuller on off, KJP looks to drive this on the up, but doesn't get the timing at all. In the air briefly towards mid-off

0.2
1lb
Ngidi to Nissanka, 1 leg bye

Another one back of a length, sliding down. Deflected off the pads down towards fine leg

0.1
•
Ngidi to Nissanka, no run

Quick and straight first up, 141kph. Angled towards leg, strikes Nissanka on the thigh guard as he looks to work it onside

Lungi Ndidi with the ball first up. Two slips in place.

6.43pm Okay the players are out in the middle. It's Pathum Nissanka and Kusal Perera out in the middle for Sri Lanka.

Umar: "Highest target chased by Srilanka in ODI world cup is 313" --- There's one record that might be safe today.

Adil: "Srilanka should target to get 330-350 to avoid a huge defeat and end up with v poor RR"

How much are we thinking Sri Lanka will get here folks?

6.40pm Welcome back folks! We're should be underway in the Sri Lankan chase, but to say they have their work cut out for them would be the mother of all understatements.
